content: Hook/middleware async ordering in ODMs: Using forEach+async without await causes hooks to race with the target operation (confirmed in Mongoat insertMany). Infinite recursion risk: async_hooks callbacks calling console.log() or async operations cause infinite recursion; synchronous logging must be used inside hook chains. Error swallowing: errors escaping async hook boundaries crash Node.js process or cause silent failures. In Mongoose, errors in pre-hooks abort the chain; in post-hooks they can be swallowed if handlers don't rethrow. Calling model methods inside hooks (e.g. .save() inside pre-save) causes infinite hook loops if not guarded with a flag.
